nameMeanings.name: Vaver
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch and North German: from Middle Dutch, Middle Low German vader ‘father, senior’; in the Middle Ages this was used as a term of address for someone who was senior in rank or age, and also to distinguish the father from the son of the same name. This surname is rare in Germany.
Altered form of   Veeder  , a surname of Dutch origin.

History: Daniel, David, William, Garret, and Paul Vader, all born in the late 1790s or early 1800s in ON, Canada, were sons of Johannes Veeder from Schenectady, NY, a descendant of Simon Volkertse Veeder, an early Dutch settler in New Netherland (see   Veeder  ).

variants.name: Veeder
meanings: Dutch: variant or an altered form of Vedder . The surname Veeder is no longer found in the Netherlands. Compare Vader 2.

History: The Veeders trace their origin to Simon Volkertse Veeder from the Netherlands, a sailor who emigrated to New Netherland in 1652 and after a few years settled in Beverwyck (now Albany, NY). Later he was also one of the original proprietors of Schenectady, NY. His descendants also bear the surname   Vader  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.
